#=======================================
# def gerer_discussion(self)
#=======================================


    def gerer_discussion(self):
        """Lit discussion.txt et répond à l'humain en texte clair"""
        if not self.discussion_file.exists():
            try:
                self.discussion_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                self.discussion_file.touch()
            except: pass
            
        try:
            with open(self.discussion_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                lignes = f.readlines()
            if not lignes:
                return False
            
            index_a_traiter = -1
            for i in range(len(lignes)-1, -1, -1):
                ligne = lignes[i].strip()
                if not ligne: continue
                # Supporte HUMAIN ou USER
                if (ligne.upper().startswith("HUMAIN") or ligne.upper().startswith("USER")) and "[TRAITÉ]" not in ligne:
                    index_a_traiter = i
                    break
            
            if index_a_traiter != -1:
                message_a_traiter = lignes[index_a_traiter].strip()
                log(f"🧠 Gemini analyse un message : {message_a_traiter[:50]}...")
                
                memoire_actuelle = ""
                if self.dashboard_file.exists():
                    try: memoire_actuelle = self.dashboard_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
                    except: pass

                prompt = f"""=== CONTEXTE DE L'ÉVOLUTION ===
DASHBOARD (Ta mémoire) :
{memoire_actuelle}

MESSAGE DE L'HUMAIN : {message_a_traiter}

=== INSTRUCTIONS ===
1. Réponds en TEXTE CLAIR à l'humain.
2. Si tu dois agir pour répondre (ex: "fais un scan"), utilise [EXECUTE], [LIRE], [ECRIRE].
3. Si tu apprends quelque chose de l'humain, mets à jour ton [DASHBOARD].
4. Utilise TOUJOURS des chemins ABSOLUS : /app/Super_Lab.

Réponds maintenant :"""
                
                reponse = self._call_gemini(prompt)
                
                if reponse:
                    lignes[index_a_traiter] = lignes[index_a_traiter].rstrip() + " [TRAITÉ]\n"
                    
                    # Nettoyage de la réponse (tags masqués à l'humain)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[EXECUTE\].*?(\[/EXECUTE\]|$)', '', reponse, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[LIRE\].*?(\[/LIRE\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[ECRIRE\].*?\|.*?(\[/ECRIRE\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[DASHBOARD\].*?(\[/DASHBOARD\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[SEARCH\].*?(\[/SEARCH\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[STAGE\s*\d+\]', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
                    reponse_propre = reponse_propre.strip()

                    if reponse_propre:
                        lignes.append(f"\nGEMINI: {reponse_propre}\n")
                    
                    with open(self.discussion_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                        f.writelines(lignes)
                    
                    # Ordres masqués
                    has_commands = any(tag in reponse for tag in ['[EXECUTE]', '[LIRE]', '[ECRIRE]', '[SEARCH]'])
                    if has_commands:
                        try:
                            with open(self.commands_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                                f.write(reponse)
                            log("📝 Ordres envoyés (via discussion)")
                        except: pass
                    
                    # Dashboard masqué
                    dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)\[/DASHBOARD\]', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    if not dash_match: # Fallback si pas de balise fermante
                        dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)$', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    
                    if dash_match:
                        try:
                            with open(self.dashboard_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                                f.write(dash_match.group(1).strip())
                            log("🧠 Dashboard mis à jour (via discussion)")
                        except: pass
                    
                    return True
                else:
                    log("❌ API N'A RIEN RENVOYÉ")
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"⚠️ Erreur dans gerer_discussion : {e}")
        return False



    def gerer_discussion(self):
        """Lit discussion.txt et rÃ©pond Ã  l'humain en texte clair"""
        if not self.discussion_file.exists():
            try:
                self.discussion_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                self.discussion_file.touch()
            except Exception as e:
                log(f"Erreur crÃ©ation discussion_file: {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                
        try:
            lignes = self.discussion_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8').splitlines(keepends=True)
            if not lignes:
                return False
            
            index_a_traiter = -1
            for i in range(len(lignes)-1, -1, -1):
                ligne = lignes[i].strip()
                if not ligne: continue
                if (ligne.upper().startswith("HUMAIN") or ligne.upper().startswith("USER")) and "[TRAITÃ‰]" not in ligne:
                    index_a_traiter = i
                    break
            
            if index_a_traiter != -1:
                message_a_traiter = lignes[index_a_traiter].strip()
                log(f"Gemini analyse un message : {message_a_traiter[:50]}...")
                
                memoire_actuelle = self.dashboard_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

                prompt = f"""=== CONTEXTE DE L'Ã‰VOLUTION ===
DASHBOARD (Ta mÃ©moire) :
{memoire_actuelle}

MESSAGE DE L'HUMAIN : {message_a_traiter}

=== INSTRUCTIONS ===
1. RÃ©ponds en TEXTE CLAIR Ã  l'humain.
2. Si tu dois agir pour rÃ©pondre (ex: "fais un scan"), utilise [EXECUTE], [LIRE], [ECRIRE].
3. Si tu apprends quelque chose de l'humain, mets Ã  jour ton [DASHBOARD].
4. Utilise TOUJOURS des chemins ABSOLUS : /app/Super_Lab.

RÃ©ponds maintenant :"""
                
                reponse = self._call_gemini(prompt)
                
                if reponse:
                    lignes[index_a_traiter] = lignes[index_a_traiter].rstrip() + " [TRAITÃ‰]\n"
                    
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[EXECUTE\].*?(\[/EXECUTE\]|$)', '', reponse, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[LIRE\].*?(\[/LIRE\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[ECRIRE\].*?\|.*?(\[/ECRIRE\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[DASHBOARD\].*?(\[/DASHBOARD\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[SEARCH\].*?(\[/SEARCH\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[STAGE\s*\d+\]', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
                    reponse_propre = reponse_propre.strip()

                    if reponse_propre:
                        lignes.append(f"\nGEMINI: {reponse_propre}\n")
                    
                    self.discussion_file.write_text("".join(lignes), encoding='utf-8')
                    
                    has_commands = any(tag in reponse for tag in ['[EXECUTE]', '[LIRE]', '[ECRIRE]', '[SEARCH]'])
                    if has_commands:
                        try:
                            self.commands_file.write_text(reponse, encoding="utf-8")
                            log("Ordres envoyÃ©s (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur Ã©criture commands_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                    
                    dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)\[/DASHBOARD\]', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    if not dash_match:
                        dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)$', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    
                    if dash_match:
                        try:
                            self.dashboard_file.write_text(dash_match.group(1).strip(), encoding="utf-8")
                            log("Dashboard mis Ã  jour (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur Ã©criture dashboard_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                    
                    self._sauvegarder_contexte("HUMAIN", message_a_traiter, reponse_propre)
                    return True
                else:
                    log("API N'A RIEN RENVOYÃ‰", level="warning")
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"Erreur dans gerer_discussion : {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
        return False



    def gerer_discussion(self):
        """Lit discussion.txt et répond à l'humain en texte clair"""
        if not self.discussion_file.exists():
            try:
                self.discussion_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                self.discussion_file.touch()
            except Exception as e:
                log(f"Erreur création discussion_file: {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                
        try:
            lignes = self.discussion_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8').splitlines(keepends=True)
            if not lignes:
                return False
            
            index_a_traiter = -1
            for i in range(len(lignes)-1, -1, -1):
                ligne = lignes[i].strip()
                if not ligne: continue
                if (ligne.upper().startswith("HUMAIN") or ligne.upper().startswith("USER")) and "[TRAITÉ]" not in ligne:
                    index_a_traiter = i
                    break
            
            if index_a_traiter != -1:
                message_a_traiter = lignes[index_a_traiter].strip()
                log(f"Gemini analyse un message : {message_a_traiter[:50]}...")
                
                memoire_actuelle = self.dashboard_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

                prompt = f"""=== CONTEXTE DE L'ÉVOLUTION ===
DASHBOARD (Ta mémoire) :
{memoire_actuelle}

MESSAGE DE L'HUMAIN : {message_a_traiter}

=== INSTRUCTIONS ===
1. Réponds en TEXTE CLAIR à l'humain.
2. Si tu dois agir pour répondre (ex: "fais un scan"), utilise [EXECUTE], [LIRE], [ECRIRE].
3. Si tu apprends quelque chose de l'humain, mets à jour ton [DASHBOARD].
4. Utilise TOUJOURS des chemins ABSOLUS : /app/Super_Lab.

Réponds maintenant :"""
                
                reponse = self._call_gemini(prompt)
                
                if reponse:
                    lignes[index_a_traiter] = lignes[index_a_traiter].rstrip() + " [TRAITÉ]\n"
                    
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[EXECUTE\].*?(\[/EXECUTE\]|$)', '', reponse, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[LIRE\].*?(\[/LIRE\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[ECRIRE\].*?\|.*?(\[/ECRIRE\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[DASHBOARD\].*?(\[/DASHBOARD\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[SEARCH\].*?(\[/SEARCH\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[STAGE\s*\d+\]', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
                    reponse_propre = reponse_propre.strip()

                    if reponse_propre:
                        lignes.append(f"\nGEMINI: {reponse_propre}\n")
                    
                    self.discussion_file.write_text("".join(lignes), encoding='utf-8')
                    
                    has_commands = any(tag in reponse for tag in ['[EXECUTE]', '[LIRE]', '[ECRIRE]', '[SEARCH]'])
                    if has_commands:
                        try:
                            self.commands_file.write_text(reponse, encoding="utf-8")
                            log("Ordres envoyés (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur écriture commands_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                    
                    dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)\[/DASHBOARD\]', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    if not dash_match:
                        dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)$', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    
                    if dash_match:
                        try:
                            self.dashboard_file.write_text(dash_match.group(1).strip(), encoding="utf-8")
                            log("Dashboard mis à jour (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur écriture dashboard_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                    
                    self._sauvegarder_contexte("HUMAIN", message_a_traiter, reponse_propre)
                    return True
                else:
                    log("API N'A RIEN RENVOYÉ", level="warning")
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"Erreur dans gerer_discussion : {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
        return False



    def gerer_discussion(self):
        """Lit discussion.txt et répond à l'humain en texte clair"""
        if not self.discussion_file.exists():
            try:
                self.discussion_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                self.discussion_file.touch()
            except Exception as e:
                log(f"Erreur création discussion_file: {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)

            lignes = self.discussion_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8').splitlines(keepends=True)

                        try:
                            self.dashboard_file.write_text(dash_match.group(1).strip(), encoding="utf-8")
                            log("Dashboard mis à jour (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur écriture dashboard_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                    
                    self._sauvegarder_contexte("HUMAIN", message_a_traiter, reponse_propre)
                    return True
                else:
                    log("API N'A RIEN RENVOYÉ", level="warning")
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"Erreur dans gerer_discussion : {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
        return False



    def gerer_discussion(self):
        """Lit discussion.txt et répond à l'humain ou à l'algo (Version Blindée)"""
        if not self.discussion_file.exists(): 
            self.discussion_file.touch()
        try:
            with open(self.discussion_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f: 
                lignes = f.readlines()
            if not lignes: 
                return
            
            index_a_traiter = -1
            for i in range(len(lignes)-1, -1, -1):
                ligne = lignes[i].strip()
                if not ligne: 
                    continue
                
                if (ligne.upper().startswith("HUMAIN") or ligne.upper().startswith("ALGO")) and "[TRAITÉ]" not in ligne:
                    index_a_traiter = i
                    break
            
            if index_a_traiter != -1:
                message_a_traiter = lignes[index_a_traiter].strip()
                log(f"🧠 Gemini analyse un message : {message_a_traiter[:50]}...")
                
                # 🔍 DEBUG
                prompt_complet_test = f"{self.system_prompt}\n\nMessage entrant :\n{message_a_traiter}"
                print(f"\n🔵 PROMPT ENVOYÉ (début): {prompt_complet_test[:200]}")
                print(f"📏 Longueur totale: {len(prompt_complet_test)} caractères")
                
                reponse = self._call_gemini(f"Message entrant :\n{message_a_traiter}")
                
                print(f"🟢 RÉPONSE REÇUE: {reponse[:100] if reponse else 'RIEN'}")
                
                if reponse:
                    with open(self.discussion_file, 'a', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                        f.write(f"\nGEMINI: {reponse}\n")
                    
                    lignes[index_a_traiter] = lignes[index_a_traiter].rstrip() + " [TRAITÉ]\n"
                    with open(self.discussion_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                        f.writelines(lignes)
                    
                    self.executer_volonte_gemini(reponse)
                else:
                    print("❌ API N'A RIEN RENVOYÉ")
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"⚠️ Erreur dans gerer_discussion : {e}")



    def gerer_discussion(self):
        """Lit discussion.txt et répond à l'humain en texte clair"""
        if not self.discussion_file.exists():
            try:
                self.discussion_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                self.discussion_file.touch()
            except Exception as e:

                return False

                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[DASHBOARD\].*?(\[/DASHBOARD\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[SEARCH\].*?(\[/SEARCH\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[STAGE\s*\d+\]', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
                    reponse_propre = reponse_propre.strip()

                    if reponse_propre:
                        lignes.append(f"\nGEMINI: {reponse_propre}\n")
                    
                    self.discussion_file.write_text("".join(lignes), encoding='utf-8')

                    if has_commands:
                        try:
                            self.commands_file.write_text(reponse, encoding="utf-8")
                            log("Ordres envoyés (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur écriture commands_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                    
                    dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)\[/DASHBOARD\]', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    if not dash_match:
                        dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)$', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    
                    if dash_match:
                        try:
                            self.dashboard_file.write_text(dash_match.group(1).strip(), encoding="utf-8")
                            log("Dashboard mis à jour (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur écriture dashboard_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                    
                    self._sauvegarder_contexte("HUMAIN", message_a_traiter, reponse_propre)
                    return True
                else:
                    log("API N'A RIEN RENVOYÉ", level="warning")
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"Erreur dans gerer_discussion : {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
        return False

                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[ECRIRE\].*?\|.*?(\[/ECRIRE\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[DASHBOARD\].*?(\[/DASHBOARD\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[SEARCH\].*?(\[/SEARCH\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[STAGE\s*\d+\]', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
                    reponse_propre = reponse_propre.strip()

                    if reponse_propre:
                        lignes.append(f"\nGEMINI: {reponse_propre}\n")
                    
                    self.discussion_file.write_text("".join(lignes), encoding='utf-8')
                    
                    has_commands = any(tag in reponse for tag in ['[EXECUTE]', '[LIRE]', '[ECRIRE]', '[SEARCH]'])
                    if has_commands:
                        try:
                            self.commands_file.write_text(reponse, encoding="utf-8")
                            log("Ordres envoyés (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur écriture commands_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                    
                    dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)\[/DASHBOARD\]', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    if not dash_match:
                        dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)$', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    
                    if dash_match:
                        try:
                            self.dashboard_file.write_text(dash_match.group(1).strip(), encoding="utf-8")
                            log("Dashboard mis à jour (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur écriture dashboard_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                    
                    return True
                else:
                    log("API N'A RIEN RENVOYÉ", level="warning")
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"Erreur dans gerer_discussion : {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
        return False



    def gerer_discussion(self):
        """Lit discussion.txt et répond à l'humain en texte clair"""
        if not self.discussion_file.exists():
            try:
                self.discussion_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                self.discussion_file.touch()
            except Exception as e:
                log(f"Erreur création discussion_file: {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                
        try:
            lignes = self.discussion_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8').splitlines(keepends=True)
            if not lignes:
                return False
            
            index_a_traiter = -1
            for i in range(len(lignes)-1, -1, -1):
                ligne = lignes[i].strip()
                if not ligne: continue
                if (ligne.upper().startswith("HUMAIN") or ligne.upper().startswith("USER")) and "[TRAITÉ]" not in ligne:
                    index_a_traiter = i
                    break
            
            if index_a_traiter != -1:
                message_a_traiter = lignes[index_a_traiter].strip()
                log(f"Gemini analyse un message : {message_a_traiter[:50]}...")
                
                memoire_actuelle = self.dashboard_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

                prompt = f"""=== CONTEXTE DE L'ÉVOLUTION ===
DASHBOARD (Ta mémoire) :
{memoire_actuelle}

MESSAGE DE L'HUMAIN : {message_a_traiter}

=== INSTRUCTIONS ===
1. Réponds en TEXTE CLAIR à l'humain.
2. Si tu dois agir pour répondre (ex: "fais un scan"), utilise [EXECUTE], [LIRE], [ECRIRE].
3. Si tu apprends quelque chose de l'humain, mets à jour ton [DASHBOARD].
4. Utilise TOUJOURS des chemins ABSOLUS : /app/Super_Lab.

Réponds maintenant :"""
                
                reponse = self._call_gemini(prompt)
                
                if reponse:
                    lignes[index_a_traiter] = lignes[index_a_traiter].rstrip() + " [TRAITÉ]\n"
                    
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[EXECUTE\].*?(\[/EXECUTE\]|$)', '', reponse, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[LIRE\].*?(\[/LIRE\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[ECRIRE\].*?\|.*?(\[/ECRIRE\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[DASHBOARD\].*?(\[/DASHBOARD\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[SEARCH\].*?(\[/SEARCH\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[STAGE\s*\d+\]', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
                    reponse_propre = reponse_propre.strip()

                    if reponse_propre:
                        lignes.append(f"\nGEMINI: {reponse_propre}\n")
                    
                    self.discussion_file.write_text("".join(lignes), encoding='utf-8')
                    
                    has_commands = any(tag in reponse for tag in ['[EXECUTE]', '[LIRE]', '[ECRIRE]', '[SEARCH]'])
                    if has_commands:
                        try:
                            self.commands_file.write_text(reponse, encoding="utf-8")
                            log("Ordres envoyés (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur écriture commands_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                    
                    dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)\[/DASHBOARD\]', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    if not dash_match:
                        dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)$', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    
                    if dash_match:
                        try:
                            self.dashboard_file.write_text(dash_match.group(1).strip(), encoding="utf-8")
                            log("Dashboard mis à jour (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur écriture dashboard_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                    
                    self._sauvegarder_contexte("HUMAIN", message_a_traiter, reponse_propre)
                    return True
                else:
                    log("API N'A RIEN RENVOYÉ", level="warning")
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"Erreur dans gerer_discussion : {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
        return False



    def gerer_discussion(self):
        """Lit discussion.txt et répond à l'humain en texte clair"""
        if not self.discussion_file.exists():
            try:
                self.discussion_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                self.discussion_file.touch()
            except: pass
            
        try:
            with open(self.discussion_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                lignes = f.readlines()
            if not lignes:
                return False
            
            index_a_traiter = -1
            for i in range(len(lignes)-1, -1, -1):
                ligne = lignes[i].strip()
                if not ligne: continue
                # Supporte HUMAIN ou USER
                if (ligne.upper().startswith("HUMAIN") or ligne.upper().startswith("USER")) and "[TRAITÉ]" not in ligne:
                    index_a_traiter = i
                    break
            
            if index_a_traiter != -1:
                message_a_traiter = lignes[index_a_traiter].strip()
                log(f"🧠 Gemini analyse un message : {message_a_traiter[:50]}...")
                
                memoire_actuelle = ""
                if self.dashboard_file.exists():
                    try: memoire_actuelle = self.dashboard_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
                    except: pass

                prompt = f"""=== CONTEXTE DE L'ÉVOLUTION ===
DASHBOARD (Ta mémoire) :
{memoire_actuelle}

MESSAGE DE L'HUMAIN : {message_a_traiter}

=== INSTRUCTIONS ===
1. Réponds en TEXTE CLAIR à l'humain.
2. Si tu dois agir pour répondre (ex: "fais un scan"), utilise [EXECUTE], [LIRE], [ECRIRE].
3. Si tu apprends quelque chose de l'humain, mets à jour ton [DASHBOARD].
4. Utilise TOUJOURS des chemins ABSOLUS : /app/Super_Lab.

Réponds maintenant :"""
                
                reponse = self._call_gemini(prompt)
                
                if reponse:
                    lignes[index_a_traiter] = lignes[index_a_traiter].rstrip() + " [TRAITÉ]\n"
                    
                    # Nettoyage de la réponse (tags masqués à l'humain)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[EXECUTE\].*?(\[/EXECUTE\]|$)', '', reponse, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[LIRE\].*?(\[/LIRE\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[ECRIRE\].*?\|.*?(\[/ECRIRE\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[DASHBOARD\].*?(\[/DASHBOARD\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[SEARCH\].*?(\[/SEARCH\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[STAGE\s*\d+\]', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
                    reponse_propre = reponse_propre.strip()

                    if reponse_propre:
                        lignes.append(f"\nGEMINI: {reponse_propre}\n")
                    
                    with open(self.discussion_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                        f.writelines(lignes)
                    
                    # Ordres masqués
                    has_commands = any(tag in reponse for tag in ['[EXECUTE]', '[LIRE]', '[ECRIRE]', '[SEARCH]'])
                    if has_commands:
                        try:
                            with open(self.commands_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                                f.write(reponse)
                            log("📝 Ordres envoyés (via discussion)")
                        except: pass
                    
                    # Dashboard masqué
                    dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)\[/DASHBOARD\]', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    if not dash_match: # Fallback si pas de balise fermante
                        dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)$', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    
                    if dash_match:
                        try:
                            with open(self.dashboard_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                                f.write(dash_match.group(1).strip())
                            log("🧠 Dashboard mis à jour (via discussion)")
                        except: pass
                    
                    return True
                else:
                    log("❌ API N'A RIEN RENVOYÉ")
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"⚠️ Erreur dans gerer_discussion : {e}")
        return False



    def gerer_discussion(self):
        if not self.discussion_file.exists(): return
        if time.time() - self.last_call < self.min_interval: return
        try:
            txt = self.discussion_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
            lignes = txt.splitlines()
            idx = -1
            # On cherche UNIQUEMENT les lignes HUMAIN non traitées
            for i in range(len(lignes)-1, -1, -1):
                if "HUMAIN:" in lignes[i].upper() and "[TRAITÉ]" not in lignes[i]:
                    idx = i; break
            
            if idx != -1:
                msg = lignes[idx]
                prompt = f"MESSAGE HUMAIN: {msg}\n\nRéponds brièvement et prends une initiative technique."
                rep = self._call_gemma(prompt)
                if rep:
                    lignes[idx] += " [TRAITÉ]"
                    clean_rep = re.sub(r'\[.*?\]', '', rep, flags=re.DOTALL).strip()
                    # On nettoie pour ne pas répéter le prompt
                    clean_rep = clean_rep.replace(msg, "").strip()
                    new_txt = "\n".join(lignes) + f"\n\nGEMMA: {clean_rep}\n"
                    self.discussion_file.write_text(new_txt, encoding='utf-8')
                    self._traiter(rep)
        except: pass



    def gerer_discussion(self):
        if not self.discussion_file.exists(): return False
        if time.time() - self.last_call < self.min_interval: return False
        try:
            txt = self.discussion_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
            lignes = txt.splitlines()
            idx = -1
            for i in range(len(lignes)-1, -1, -1):
                if ("HUMAIN" in lignes[i].upper() or "USER" in lignes[i].upper()) and "[TRAITÉ]" not in lignes[i]:
                    idx = i; break
            if idx != -1:
                msg = lignes[idx]
                log(f"🧠 Discussion: {msg[:50]}...")
                try: mem = self.dashboard_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
                except: mem = ""
                prompt = f"DASHBOARD: {mem}\n\nMESSAGE HUMAIN: {msg}\n\nRéponse (texte clair) :"
                rep = self._call_gemini(prompt)
                if rep:
                    lignes[idx] += " [TRAITÉ]"
                    clean_rep = re.sub(r'\[.*?\]', '', rep, flags=re.DOTALL).strip()
                    new_txt = "\n".join(lignes) + f"\n\nGEMINI: {clean_rep}\n"
                    self.discussion_file.write_text(new_txt, encoding='utf-8')
                    self._traiter(rep)
                    return True
        except: pass
        return False



    def gerer_discussion(self):
        """Lit discussion.txt et répond à l'humain en texte clair"""
        if not self.discussion_file.exists():
            try:
                self.discussion_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                self.discussion_file.touch()
            except: pass
            
        try:
            with open(self.discussion_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                lignes = f.readlines()
            if not lignes:
                return False
            
            index_a_traiter = -1
            for i in range(len(lignes)-1, -1, -1):
                ligne = lignes[i].strip()
                if not ligne: continue
                # Supporte HUMAIN ou USER
                if (ligne.upper().startswith("HUMAIN") or ligne.upper().startswith("USER")) and "[TRAITÉ]" not in ligne:
                    index_a_traiter = i
                    break
            
            if index_a_traiter != -1:
                message_a_traiter = lignes[index_a_traiter].strip()
                log(f"🧠 Gemini analyse un message : {message_a_traiter[:50]}...")
                
                memoire_actuelle = ""
                if self.dashboard_file.exists():
                    try: memoire_actuelle = self.dashboard_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
                    except: pass

                prompt = f"""=== CONTEXTE DE L'ÉVOLUTION ===
DASHBOARD (Ta mémoire) :
{memoire_actuelle}

MESSAGE DE L'HUMAIN : {message_a_traiter}

=== INSTRUCTIONS ===
1. Réponds en TEXTE CLAIR à l'humain.
2. Si tu dois agir pour répondre (ex: "fais un scan"), utilise [EXECUTE], [LIRE], [ECRIRE].
3. Si tu apprends quelque chose de l'humain, mets à jour ton [DASHBOARD].
4. Utilise TOUJOURS des chemins ABSOLUS : /app/Super_Lab.

Réponds maintenant :"""
                
                reponse = self._call_gemini(prompt)
                
                if reponse:
                    lignes[index_a_traiter] = lignes[index_a_traiter].rstrip() + " [TRAITÉ]\n"
                    
                    # Nettoyage de la réponse (tags masqués à l'humain)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[EXECUTE\].*?(\[/EXECUTE\]|$)', '', reponse, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[LIRE\].*?(\[/LIRE\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[ECRIRE\].*?\|.*?(\[/ECRIRE\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[DASHBOARD\].*?(\[/DASHBOARD\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[SEARCH\].*?(\[/SEARCH\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[STAGE\s*\d+\]', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
                    reponse_propre = reponse_propre.strip()

                    if reponse_propre:
                        lignes.append(f"\nGEMINI: {reponse_propre}\n")
                    
                    with open(self.discussion_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                        f.writelines(lignes)
                    
                    # Ordres masqués
                    has_commands = any(tag in reponse for tag in ['[EXECUTE]', '[LIRE]', '[ECRIRE]', '[SEARCH]'])
                    if has_commands:
                        try:
                            with open(self.commands_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                                f.write(reponse)
                            log("📝 Ordres envoyés (via discussion)")
                        except: pass
                    
                    # Dashboard masqué
                    dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)\[/DASHBOARD\]', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    if not dash_match: # Fallback si pas de balise fermante
                        dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)$', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    
                    if dash_match:
                        try:
                            with open(self.dashboard_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                                f.write(dash_match.group(1).strip())
                            log("🧠 Dashboard mis à jour (via discussion)")
                        except: pass
                    
                    return True
                else:
                    log("❌ API N'A RIEN RENVOYÉ")
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"⚠️ Erreur dans gerer_discussion : {e}")
        return False

                    if not dash_match:

                        try:
                            self.dashboard_file.write_text(dash_match.group(1).strip(), encoding="utf-8")
                            log("Dashboard mis à jour (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur écriture dashboard_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)

        except Exception as e:
            log(f"Erreur dans gerer_discussion : {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
        return False

4. Utilise TOUJOURS des chemins ABSOLUS : /app/Super_Lab.

Réponds maintenant :"""
                
                reponse = self._call_gemini(prompt)
                
                if reponse:
                    lignes[index_a_traiter] = lignes[index_a_traiter].rstrip() + " [TRAITÉ]\n"
                    
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[EXECUTE\].*?(\[/EXECUTE\]|$)', '', reponse, flags=re.DOTALL)

                            log("Ordres envoyés (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur écriture commands_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                    
                    dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)\[/DASHBOARD\]', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    if not dash_match:
                        dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)$', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    
                    if dash_match:
                        try:
                            self.dashboard_file.write_text(dash_match.group(1).strip(), encoding="utf-8")
                            log("Dashboard mis à jour (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur écriture dashboard_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                    
                    self._sauvegarder_contexte("HUMAIN", message_a_traiter, reponse_propre)
                    return True
                else:
                    log("API N'A RIEN RENVOYÉ", level="warning")
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"Erreur dans gerer_discussion : {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
        return False



    def gerer_discussion(self):
        """Répond aux messages de l'humain."""
        if not self.discussion_file.exists(): return False
        content = self.discussion_file.read_text()
        if "USER:" in content and "[TRAITÉ]" not in content:
            log("Discussion : Gemini prépare une réponse...")
            reponse = self._call_gemini(content)
            if reponse:
                self.discussion_file.write_text(content + f"\nGEMINI: {reponse} [TRAITÉ]")
                return True
        return False



    def gerer_discussion(self):
        """Lit discussion.txt et répond à l'humain ou à l'algo (Version Blindée)"""
        if not self.discussion_file.exists(): 
            self.discussion_file.touch()
        try:
            with open(self.discussion_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f: 
                lignes = f.readlines()
            if not lignes: 
                return
            
            index_a_traiter = -1
            for i in range(len(lignes)-1, -1, -1):
                ligne = lignes[i].strip()
                if not ligne: 
                    continue
                
                if (ligne.upper().startswith("HUMAIN") or ligne.upper().startswith("ALGO")) and "[TRAITÉ]" not in ligne:
                    index_a_traiter = i
                    break
            
            if index_a_traiter != -1:
                message_a_traiter = lignes[index_a_traiter].strip()
                log(f"🧠 Gemini analyse un message : {message_a_traiter[:50]}...")
                
                # 🔍 DEBUG
                prompt_complet_test = f"{self.system_prompt}\n\nMessage entrant :\n{message_a_traiter}"
                print(f"\n🔵 PROMPT ENVOYÉ (début): {prompt_complet_test[:200]}")
                print(f"📏 Longueur totale: {len(prompt_complet_test)} caractères")
                
                reponse = self._call_gemini(f"Message entrant :\n{message_a_traiter}")
                
                print(f"🟢 RÉPONSE REÇUE: {reponse[:100] if reponse else 'RIEN'}")
                
                if reponse:
                    with open(self.discussion_file, 'a', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                        f.write(f"\nGEMINI: {reponse}\n")
                    
                    lignes[index_a_traiter] = lignes[index_a_traiter].rstrip() + " [TRAITÉ]\n"
                    with open(self.discussion_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                        f.writelines(lignes)
                    
                    self.executer_volonte_gemini(reponse)
                else:
                    print("❌ API N'A RIEN RENVOYÉ")
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"⚠️ Erreur dans gerer_discussion : {e}")



    def gerer_discussion(self):
        """Lit discussion.txt et répond à l'humain en texte clair"""
        if not self.discussion_file.exists():
            try:
                self.discussion_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
                self.discussion_file.touch()
            except Exception as e:
                log(f"Erreur création discussion_file: {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                
        try:
            lignes = self.discussion_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8').splitlines(keepends=True)
            if not lignes:
                return False
            
            index_a_traiter = -1
            for i in range(len(lignes)-1, -1, -1):
                ligne = lignes[i].strip()
                if not ligne: continue
                if (ligne.upper().startswith("HUMAIN") or ligne.upper().startswith("USER")) and "[TRAITÉ]" not in ligne:
                    index_a_traiter = i
                    break
            
            if index_a_traiter != -1:
                message_a_traiter = lignes[index_a_traiter].strip()
                log(f"Gemini analyse un message : {message_a_traiter[:50]}...")
                
                memoire_actuelle = self.dashboard_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

                prompt = f"""=== CONTEXTE DE L'ÉVOLUTION ===
DASHBOARD (Ta mémoire) :
{memoire_actuelle}

MESSAGE DE L'HUMAIN : {message_a_traiter}

=== INSTRUCTIONS ===
1. Réponds en TEXTE CLAIR à l'humain.
2. Si tu dois agir pour répondre (ex: "fais un scan"), utilise [EXECUTE], [LIRE], [ECRIRE].
3. Si tu apprends quelque chose de l'humain, mets à jour ton [DASHBOARD].
4. Utilise TOUJOURS des chemins ABSOLUS : /app/Super_Lab.

Réponds maintenant :"""
                
                reponse = self._call_gemini(prompt)
                
                if reponse:
                    lignes[index_a_traiter] = lignes[index_a_traiter].rstrip() + " [TRAITÉ]\n"
                    
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[EXECUTE\].*?(\[/EXECUTE\]|$)', '', reponse, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[LIRE\].*?(\[/LIRE\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[ECRIRE\].*?\|.*?(\[/ECRIRE\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[DASHBOARD\].*?(\[/DASHBOARD\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[SEARCH\].*?(\[/SEARCH\]|$)', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.DOTALL)
                    reponse_propre = re.sub(r'\[STAGE\s*\d+\]', '', reponse_propre, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
                    reponse_propre = reponse_propre.strip()

                    if reponse_propre:
                        lignes.append(f"\nGEMINI: {reponse_propre}\n")
                    
                    self.discussion_file.write_text("".join(lignes), encoding='utf-8')
                    
                    has_commands = any(tag in reponse for tag in ['[EXECUTE]', '[LIRE]', '[ECRIRE]', '[SEARCH]'])
                    if has_commands:
                        try:
                            self.commands_file.write_text(reponse, encoding="utf-8")
                            log("Ordres envoyés (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur écriture commands_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                    
                    dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)\[/DASHBOARD\]', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    if not dash_match:
                        dash_match = re.search(r'\[DASHBOARD\](.*?)$', reponse, re.DOTALL)
                    
                    if dash_match:
                        try:
                            self.dashboard_file.write_text(dash_match.group(1).strip(), encoding="utf-8")
                            log("Dashboard mis à jour (via discussion)")
                        except Exception as e:
                            log(f"Erreur écriture dashboard_file (discussion): {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
                    
                    self._sauvegarder_contexte("HUMAIN", message_a_traiter, reponse_propre)
                    return True
                else:
                    log("API N'A RIEN RENVOYÉ", level="warning")
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"Erreur dans gerer_discussion : {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
        return False

def get_alternatives_apprises_ci(commande):
    """RÃ©cupÃ¨re les alternatives apprises."""
    solutions = []
    try:
        with open(EVOLUT_HOME / "solutions_apprises.log", 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            content = f.read()
            if commande in content:
                solutions = extraire_solutions_du_log_ci(content, commande)
    except Exception as e:
        log(f"[LOCAL_CI] Erreur lecture solutions_apprises.log: {e}", level="error")
    return solutions

    def get_commandes_apprises(self):
        """Liste des commandes apprises - ✅ NOUVELLE FONCTION DE LA VERSION 2"""
        try:
            with open(LEARNING_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                return len(f.readlines())
        except:
            return 0



    def get_commandes_apprises(self):
        """Liste des commandes apprises et les persiste dans SQLite."""
        try:
            if not LEARNING_FILE.exists():
                return 0

            with open(LEARNING_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                lines = f.readlines()
            
            commandes_apprises_count = len(lines)

            # Persister les commandes apprises dans SQLite (exemple simplifiÃ©)
            conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_file)
            cursor = conn.cursor()
            for line in lines:
                match = re.match(r"\[(.*?)\] PATTERN: (.*)", line)
                if match:
                    timestamp_str, commande = match.groups()
                    cursor.execute(
                        "INSERT INTO commandes_apprises_historique (timestamp, commande) VALUES (?, ?)",
                        (datetime.strptime(timestamp_str, '%F %T').timestamp(), commande.strip())
                    )
            conn.commit()
            conn.close()

            return commandes_apprises_count
        except FileNotFoundError:
            return 0
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"âŒ Erreur get_commandes_apprises: {e}", level="error")
            return 0

    def get_commandes_apprises(self):

            conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_file)

                    timestamp_str, commande = match.groups()
                    cursor.execute(
                        "INSERT INTO commandes_apprises_historique (timestamp, commande) VALUES (?, ?)",
                        (datetime.strptime(timestamp_str, '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S').timestamp(), commande.strip())
                    )

    def get_commandes_apprises(self):
        """Liste des commandes apprises et les persiste dans SQLite."""
        try:
            if not LEARNING_FILE.exists():
                return 0

            with open(LEARNING_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                lines = f.readlines()
            
            commandes_apprises_count = len(lines)

            # Persister les commandes apprises dans SQLite (exemple simplifié)
            conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_file)
            cursor = conn.cursor()
            for line in lines:
                match = re.match(r"\[(.*?)\] PATTERN: (.*)", line)
                if match:
                    timestamp_str, commande = match.groups()
                    cursor.execute(
                        "INSERT INTO commandes_apprises_historique (timestamp, commande) VALUES (?, ?)",
                        (datetime.strptime(timestamp_str, '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S').timestamp(), commande.strip())
                    )
            conn.commit()
            conn.close()

            return commandes_apprises_count
        except FileNotFoundError:
            return 0
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"❌ Erreur get_commandes_apprises: {e}", level="error")
            return 0



    def get_commandes_apprises(self):
        """Liste des commandes apprises et les persiste dans SQLite."""
        try:
            if not LEARNING_FILE.exists():
                return 0

            with open(LEARNING_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                lines = f.readlines()
            
            commandes_apprises_count = len(lines)

            # Persister les commandes apprises dans SQLite (exemple simplifié)
            conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_file)
            cursor = conn.cursor()
            for line in lines:
                match = re.match(r"\[(.*?)\] PATTERN: (.*)", line)
                if match:
                    timestamp_str, commande = match.groups()
                    cursor.execute(
                        "INSERT INTO commandes_apprises_historique (timestamp, commande) VALUES (?, ?)",
                        (datetime.strptime(timestamp_str, '%F %T').timestamp(), commande.strip())
                    )
            conn.commit()
            conn.close()

            return commandes_apprises_count
        except FileNotFoundError:
            return 0
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"❌ Erreur get_commandes_apprises: {e}", level="error")
            return 0



    def get_commandes_apprises(self):
        """Liste des commandes apprises - ✅ NOUVELLE FONCTION DE LA VERSION 2"""
        try:
            with open(LEARNING_FILE, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                return len(f.readlines())
        except:
            return 0

    def get_dernieres_reussites(self):
        """Extrait les dernières réussites des archives."""
        try:
            # Utilisation de pandas pour un comptage plus robuste
            df_archives = pd.read_csv(ARCHIVE_FILE, sep='=== ARCHIVE', header=None, engine='python', skipinitialspace=True)
            successful_entries = df_archives[df_archives[0].str.contains("✅|RÉUSSITE", na=False)]
            return len(successful_entries)
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"Erreur get_dernieres_reussites: {e}", level="error")
            return 0

    def get_echecs_recents(self):
        """Extrait les échecs récents - LES DEUX VERSIONS SONT IDENTIQUES"""

    def get_echecs_recents(self):
        """Extrait les Ã©checs rÃ©cents et les persiste dans SQLite."""
        try:
            if not (EVOLUT_HOME / "failed_commands.log").exists():
                return 0

            with open(EVOLUT_HOME / "failed_commands.log", 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                lines = f.readlines()
            
            echecs_count = len(lines)

            # Persister les Ã©checs dans SQLite (exemple simplifiÃ©)
            conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_file)
            cursor = conn.cursor()
            for line in lines:
                match = re.match(r"\[(.*?)\] CMD: (.*?) \| ERR: (.*)", line)
                if match:
                    timestamp_str, commande, erreur = match.groups()
                    cursor.execute(
                        "INSERT INTO echecs_historique (timestamp, commande, erreur) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
                        (datetime.strptime(timestamp_str, '%F %T').timestamp(), commande.strip(), erreur.strip())
                    )
            conn.commit()
            conn.close()

            return echecs_count
        except FileNotFoundError:
            return 0
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"âŒ Erreur get_echecs_recents: {e}", level="error")
            return 0

                    cursor.execute(
                        "INSERT INTO echecs_historique (timestamp, commande, erreur) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
                        (datetime.strptime(timestamp_str, '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S').timestamp(), commande.strip(), erreur.strip())
                    )
            conn.commit()
            conn.close()

            return echecs_count
        except FileNotFoundError:
            return 0
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"❌ Erreur get_echecs_recents: {e}", level="error")
            return 0



    def get_echecs_recents(self):
        """Extrait les échecs récents et les persiste dans SQLite."""
        try:
            if not (EVOLUT_HOME / "failed_commands.log").exists():
                return 0

            with open(EVOLUT_HOME / "failed_commands.log", 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                lines = f.readlines()
            
            echecs_count = len(lines)

            # Persister les échecs dans SQLite (exemple simplifié)
            conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_file)
            cursor = conn.cursor()
            for line in lines:
                match = re.match(r"\[(.*?)\] CMD: (.*?) \| ERR: (.*)", line)
                if match:
                    timestamp_str, commande, erreur = match.groups()
                    cursor.execute(
                        "INSERT INTO echecs_historique (timestamp, commande, erreur) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
                        (datetime.strptime(timestamp_str, '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S').timestamp(), commande.strip(), erreur.strip())
                    )
            conn.commit()
            conn.close()

            return echecs_count
        except FileNotFoundError:
            return 0
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"❌ Erreur get_echecs_recents: {e}", level="error")
            return 0



    def get_echecs_recents(self):
        """Extrait les échecs récents et les persiste dans SQLite."""
        try:
            if not (EVOLUT_HOME / "failed_commands.log").exists():
                return 0

            with open(EVOLUT_HOME / "failed_commands.log", 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
                lines = f.readlines()
            
            echecs_count = len(lines)

            # Persister les échecs dans SQLite (exemple simplifié)
            conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_file)
            cursor = conn.cursor()
            for line in lines:
                match = re.match(r"\[(.*?)\] CMD: (.*?) \| ERR: (.*)", line)
                if match:
                    timestamp_str, commande, erreur = match.groups()
                    cursor.execute(
                        "INSERT INTO echecs_historique (timestamp, commande, erreur) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
                        (datetime.strptime(timestamp_str, '%F %T').timestamp(), commande.strip(), erreur.strip())
                    )
            conn.commit()
            conn.close()

            return echecs_count
        except FileNotFoundError:
            return 0
        except Exception as e:
            log(f"❌ Erreur get_echecs_recents: {e}", level="error")
            return 0









#=======================================
# def get_environment_info()
#=======================================


def get_environment_info():
    """Récupère des informations d'environnement pour le log"""
    try:
        user = subprocess.run("whoami", shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5).stdout.strip()
        cwd = subprocess.run("pwd", shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5).stdout.strip()
        return f"User: {user}, CWD: {cwd}"
    except:
        return "Info environnement non disponible"








#=======================================
# def get_lang_path(lang)
#=======================================


def get_lang_path(lang):
    """Retourne le chemin du dossier dédié à un langage."""
    return EVOLUT_HOME / lang.lower()







#=======================================
# def get_legacy_missions(self)
#=======================================


    def get_legacy_missions(self):
        """Fusion des 30 générateurs de missions historiques."""
        return [
            "find /system -type f -exec sha256sum {} \\; 2>/dev/null | head -10",
            "netstat -tunap | awk '{print $4,$5}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | head -10",
            "ps aux --sort=-%mem | head -10",
            "pm list packages -3 | cut -d: -f2 | xargs -n1 dumpsys package",
            "getprop | grep -E 'dns|host|ip|wifi'",
            "cat /proc/net/arp",
            "df -h | grep -v tmpfs"
        ]

#=======================================
# def get_related_concepts(self, concept_name, relation_type=None)
#=======================================


    def get_related_concepts(self, concept_name, relation_type=None):
        related = []
        if self.graph.has_node(concept_name):
            for neighbor in self.graph.neighbors(concept_name):
                for _, _, data in self.graph.edges(concept_name, neighbor, data=True):
                    if relation_type is None or data.get('type') == relation_type:
                        related.append((neighbor, data.get('type')))
        return related

                    if relation_type is None or data.get('type') == relation_type:
                        related.append((neighbor, data.get('type')))
        return related

#=======================================
# def get_signature(cmd)
#=======================================


    def get_signature(cmd):
        # Sépare la commande par les opérateurs logiques et les pipes, prend les 3 premiers éléments
        parts = re.split(r'\s+|\||&&|;', cmd.strip())
        # Garde les parties non vides et significatives
        significant_parts = [p for p in parts if p and not p.startswith('-')][:3]
        return tuple(significant_parts)

    signature_candidate = get_signature(commande_candidate)
    if not signature_candidate:
        return True # Éviter les commandes vides

    try:
        # On lit le fichier courant pour vérifier les fonctions existantes
        with open(__file__, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            code_complet = f.read()

        # Trouve toutes les commandes déjà intégrées dans le code
        commandes_existantes = re.findall(r"execute_commande_securisee\((.*?)\)", code_complet)

        for cmd_str in commandes_existantes:
            # Nettoie la chaîne (enlève les guillemets, etc.)
            try:
                # Tente d'évaluer la chaîne pour obtenir la commande réelle
                cmd_existante = eval(cmd_str)
                if isinstance(cmd_existante, str):
                    signature_existante = get_signature(cmd_existante)
                    if signature_candidate == signature_existante:
                        log(f"🚫 Signature de commande déjà intégrée: {signature_candidate}")
                        return True
            except:
                # Si eval échoue, c'est probablement une variable, on ignore
                continue
    except Exception as e:
        log(f"⚠️ Erreur lors de la vérification de signature: {e}")
        return True # En cas de doute, on évite de créer un doublon

    return False



    def get_signature(cmd):
        parts = re.split(r'\s+|\||&&|;', cmd.strip())
        significant_parts = [p for p in parts if p and not p.startswith('-')][:3]
        return tuple(significant_parts)

    signature_candidate = get_signature(commande_candidate)
    if not signature_candidate:
        return True

    try:
        with open(__file__, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            code_complet = f.read()

        commandes_existantes = re.findall(r"execute_commande_securisee\((.*?)\)", code_complet)

        for cmd_str in commandes_existantes:
            try:
                cmd_existante = eval(cmd_str)
                if isinstance(cmd_existante, str):
                    signature_existante = get_signature(cmd_existante)
                    if signature_candidate == signature_existante:
                        log(f"Signature de commande dÃ©jÃ  intÃ©grÃ©e: {signature_candidate}", level="info")
                        return True
            except Exception as e:
                log(f"Erreur lors de l'Ã©valuation de la commande existante '{cmd_str}': {e}", level="debug")
                continue
    except Exception as e:
        log(f"Erreur lors de la vÃ©rification de signature: {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
        return True

    # Comparaison sÃ©mantique des commandes avec VectorMemory
    try:
        similar_entries = vector_memory.search_similar(commande_candidate, top_k=5)
        for entry in similar_entries:
            if entry['metadata'].get('type') == 'commande' and entry['distance'] < 0.1: # Seuil de similaritÃ©
                log(f"Commande sÃ©mantiquement similaire dÃ©jÃ  intÃ©grÃ©e via VectorMemory: '{commande_candidate}' vs '{entry['text_content']}' (dist: {entry['distance']:.2f})", level="info")
                return True
    except Exception as e:
        log(f"Erreur lors de la vÃ©rification de similaritÃ© sÃ©mantique via VectorMemory: {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)

    return False



    def get_signature(cmd):
        parts = re.split(r'\s+|\||&&|;', cmd.strip())
        significant_parts = [p for p in parts if p and not p.startswith('-')][:3]
        return tuple(significant_parts)

    signature_candidate = get_signature(commande_candidate)
    if not signature_candidate:
        return True

    try:
        with open(__file__, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            code_complet = f.read()

        commandes_existantes = re.findall(r"execute_commande_securisee\((.*?)\)", code_complet)

        for cmd_str in commandes_existantes:
            try:
                cmd_existante = eval(cmd_str)
                if isinstance(cmd_existante, str):
                    signature_existante = get_signature(cmd_existante)
                    if signature_candidate == signature_existante:
                        return True
            except Exception:
                continue
    except Exception as e:
        log(f"Erreur lors de la vérification de signature: {e}", level="error")
        return True

    try:
        similar_entries = vector_memory.search_similar(commande_candidate, top_k=1)
        if similar_entries and similar_entries[0]['distance'] < 0.1:
            return True
    except Exception:
        pass

    return False



    def get_signature(cmd):
        # Sépare la commande par les opérateurs logiques et les pipes, prend les 3 premiers éléments
        parts = re.split(r'\s+|\||&&|;', cmd.strip())
        # Garde les parties non vides et significatives
        significant_parts = [p for p in parts if p and not p.startswith('-')][:3]
        return tuple(significant_parts)

    signature_candidate = get_signature(commande_candidate)
    if not signature_candidate:
        return True # Éviter les commandes vides

    try:
        with open(__file__, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            code_complet = f.read()

        # Trouve toutes les commandes déjà intégrées dans le code
        commandes_existantes = re.findall(r"execute_commande_securisee\((.*?)\)", code_complet)

        for cmd_str in commandes_existantes:
            # Nettoie la chaîne (enlève les guillemets, etc.)
            try:
                # Tente d'évaluer la chaîne pour obtenir la commande réelle
                cmd_existante = eval(cmd_str)
                if isinstance(cmd_existante, str):
                    signature_existante = get_signature(cmd_existante)
                    if signature_candidate == signature_existante:
                        log(f"🚫 Signature de commande déjà intégrée: {signature_candidate}")
                        return True
            except:
                # Si eval échoue, c'est probablement une variable, on ignore
                continue
    except Exception as e:
        log(f"⚠️ Erreur lors de la vérification de signature: {e}")
        return True # En cas de doute, on évite de créer un doublon

    return False



    def get_signature(cmd):
        parts = re.split(r'\s+|\||&&|;', cmd.strip())
        significant_parts = [p for p in parts if p and not p.startswith('-')][:3]
        return tuple(significant_parts)

    signature_candidate = get_signature(commande_candidate)
    if not signature_candidate:
        return True

    try:
        with open(__file__, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            code_complet = f.read()

        commandes_existantes = re.findall(r"execute_commande_securisee\((.*?)\)", code_complet)

        for cmd_str in commandes_existantes:
            try:
                cmd_existante = eval(cmd_str)
                if isinstance(cmd_existante, str):
                    signature_existante = get_signature(cmd_existante)
                    if signature_candidate == signature_existante:
                        log(f"Signature de commande déjà intégrée: {signature_candidate}", level="info")
                        return True
            except Exception as e:
                log(f"Erreur lors de l'évaluation de la commande existante '{cmd_str}': {e}", level="debug")
                continue
    except Exception as e:
        log(f"Erreur lors de la vérification de signature: {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)
        return True

    # Comparaison sémantique des commandes avec VectorMemory
    try:
        similar_entries = vector_memory.search_similar(commande_candidate, top_k=5)
        for entry in similar_entries:
            if entry['metadata'].get('type') == 'commande' and entry['distance'] < 0.1: # Seuil de similarité
                log(f"Commande sémantiquement similaire déjà intégrée via VectorMemory: '{commande_candidate}' vs '{entry['text_content']}' (dist: {entry['distance']:.2f})", level="info")
                return True
    except Exception as e:
        log(f"Erreur lors de la vérification de similarité sémantique via VectorMemory: {e}", level="error", exc_info=True)

    return False

        for entry in similar_entries:
            if entry['metadata'].get('type') == 'commande' and entry['distance'] < 0.1: # Seuil de similaritÃ©
                log(f"Commande sÃ©mantiquement similaire dÃ©jÃ  intÃ©grÃ©e via VectorMemory: '{commande_candidate}' vs '{entry['text_content']}' (dist: {entry['distance']:.2f})", level="info")

    def get_signature(cmd):
        # Sépare la commande par les opérateurs logiques et les pipes, prend les 3 premiers éléments
        parts = re.split(r'\s+|\||&&|;', cmd.strip())
        # Garde les parties non vides et significatives
        significant_parts = [p for p in parts if p and not p.startswith('-')][:3]
        return tuple(significant_parts)

    signature_candidate = get_signature(commande_candidate)
    if not signature_candidate:
        return True # Éviter les commandes vides

    try:
        with open(__file__, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            code_complet = f.read()

        # Trouve toutes les commandes déjà intégrées dans le code
        commandes_existantes = re.findall(r"execute_commande_securisee\((.*?)\)", code_complet)

        for cmd_str in commandes_existantes:
            # Nettoie la chaîne (enlève les guillemets, etc.)
            try:
                # Tente d'évaluer la chaîne pour obtenir la commande réelle
                cmd_existante = eval(cmd_str)
                if isinstance(cmd_existante, str):
                    signature_existante = get_signature(cmd_existante)
                    if signature_candidate == signature_existante:
                        log(f"🚫 Signature de commande déjà intégrée: {signature_candidate}")
                        return True
            except:
                # Si eval échoue, c'est probablement une variable, on ignore
                continue
    except Exception as e:
        log(f"⚠️ Erreur lors de la vérification de signature: {e}")
        return True # En cas de doute, on évite de créer un doublon

    return False

        significant_parts = [p for p in parts if p and not p.startswith('-')][:3]
        return tuple(significant_parts)

    signature_candidate = get_signature(commande_candidate)
    if not signature_candidate:
        return True

    try:
        with open(__file__, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            code_complet = f.read()

        commandes_existantes = re.findall(r"execute_commande_securisee\((.*?)\)", code_complet)

        for cmd_str in commandes_existantes:
            try:
                cmd_existante = eval(cmd_str)
                if isinstance(cmd_existante, str):
                    signature_existante = get_signature(cmd_existante)
                    if signature_candidate == signature_existante:
                        return True
            except Exception:
                continue
    except Exception as e:
        log(f"Erreur lors de la vérification de signature: {e}", level="error")
        return True

    try:
        similar_entries = vector_memory.search_similar(commande_candidate, top_k=1)
        if similar_entries and similar_entries[0]['distance'] < 0.1:
            return True
    except Exception:
        pass

    return False

#=======================================
# def _get_snippet_aleatoire(self, lang, obj)
#=======================================


    def _get_snippet_aleatoire(self, lang, obj):
        """Récupère un snippet (mémoire, fichier existant ou template)."""
        # 1. Tenter de lire dans les fichiers existants du Super_Lab (Réutilisation)
        lang_dir = EVOLUT_HOME / lang.lower()
        if lang_dir.exists():
            ext = self.matrix[lang].get("ext", ".txt")
            files = list(lang_dir.glob(f"*{ext}"))
            if files and random.random() > 0.5:
                try:
                    with open(random.choice(files), "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
                        content = f.read()
                        if content: return content
                except:
                    pass

        # 2. Tenter de lire dans la mémoire des réussites
        reussites = self.memories.get(lang, {}).get("reussites", [])
        if reussites and random.random() > 0.3:
            return random.choice(reussites)
        
        # 3. Fallback sur Template
        templates = {
            "PYTHON": f"import os; print('Hybrid Logic: {obj}')",
            "JS": f"console.log('Hybrid Logic: {obj}')",
            "C": f"printf('Hybrid Logic: {obj}\\n');",
            "RUST": f"println!('Hybrid Logic: {obj}');",
            "JAVA": f"System.out.println('Hybrid Logic: {obj}');",
            "SQL": f"SELECT '{obj}';"
        }
        return templates.get(lang, obj)

#=======================================
# def get_variants(self, name)
#=======================================


    def get_variants(self, name):
        """Récupère toutes les versions historiques d'une fonction."""
        conn = sqlite3.connect(DB_FILE)
        res = conn.execute("SELECT file, line_start FROM functions WHERE name = ?", (name,)).fetchall()
        conn.close()
        return res

    def identifier_contextes_favorables(self, succes_list):
        if not succes_list:
            return
        df_succes = pd.DataFrame([s.get('contexte_systeme', {}) for s in succes_list])
        numeric_cols = df_succes.select_dtypes(include=[np.number]).columns
        if len(numeric_cols) > 0:
            favorable_means = df_succes[numeric_cols].mean()
            log(f"ðŸ“Š Contextes favorables: {favorable_means.to_dict()}")

#=======================================
# def identifier_echecs_recurrents(self, echecs)
#=======================================


    def identifier_echecs_recurrents(self, echecs):
        """
        Identifie les échecs qui se répètent dans mêmes conditions
        """
        patterns_echec = {}

        for echec in echecs:
            cle_contexte = self.creer_cle_contexte(echec["contexte_systeme"])
            type_erreur = echec["erreur_observee"]

            if cle_contexte not in patterns_echec:
                patterns_echec[cle_contexte] = {}

            if type_erreur not in patterns_echec[cle_contexte]:
                patterns_echec[cle_contexte][type_erreur] = []

            patterns_echec[cle_contexte][type_erreur].append(echec["commande"])

        # Sauvegarder les patterns d'échec
        for contexte, erreurs in patterns_echec.items():
            for erreur, commandes in erreurs.items():
                if len(commandes) >= 2:  # Au moins 2 échecs similaires
                    self.apprendre_evitement_contexte(contexte, erreur, commandes)



    def identifier_echecs_recurrents(self, echecs):
        """
        Identifie les Ã©checs qui se rÃ©pÃ¨tent dans mÃªmes conditions en utilisant le clustering.
        """
        patterns_echec_temp = {}
        for echec in echecs:
            cle_contexte = self.creer_cle_contexte(echec["contexte_systeme"])
            type_erreur = echec["erreur_observee"]

            if cle_contexte not in patterns_echec_temp:
                patterns_echec_temp[cle_contexte] = {}
            if type_erreur not in patterns_echec_temp[cle_contexte]:
                patterns_echec_temp[cle_contexte][type_erreur] = []
            patterns_echec_temp[cle_contexte][type_erreur].append(echec["commande"])

        for contexte, erreurs in patterns_echec_temp.items():
            for erreur, commandes in erreurs.items():
                if len(commandes) >= 2:
                    self.apprendre_evitement_contexte(contexte, erreur, commandes)
